editor can complete A/B/C, generated history, and asset download
viewer can inspect but cannot mutate or create artifacts
new authenticated account resolves a writable workspace and completes benchmark A

R106 proved viewer read-only UI and local sync suppression. R107/R108 hydrated generated history from cloud artifacts and verified viewer download. R110 added backend route stress: direct viewer mutation calls are denied and request-scoped permission audit rows are persisted.
evidence.requestIdOrTraceId: R110 viewer stress: GET artifacts=200; five mutation routes=403; permission_audit_logs denied=5 allowed=1 total=6; artifact/storage counts unchanged.

tasks.expectation: Viewer session resolves on an existing workspace.

evidence.manualNote: Chrome screen showed Workspace cloud session linked, ROLE: VIEWER, shared workspace id, and top-right SYNCED on the fresh preview.

tasks.expectation: Viewer can inspect Workflow Pro state and generated history.

evidence.manualNote: Viewer inspected the shared workspace, generated history panel, and the newest durable generated-image record from the Chrome screen.
evidence.requestIdOrTraceId: GET /api/v1/artifacts status=200; generated-image count for shared workspace=3

tasks.expectation: Run/start/save/create attempt is blocked by UI or returns clean permission denied.

evidence.manualNote: Graph showed VIEWER READ ONLY and disabled mutation controls. R110 then directly stressed the backend routes: POST artifacts, POST sync operations, PUT workspace state, POST agent stream, and POST image-gen all returned 403 for the viewer.
evidence.requestIdOrTraceId: R110 preview nexus-ik5hscmen: GET artifacts=200; POST artifacts=403; POST sync=403; PUT workspace state=403; POST agent stream=403; POST image-gen=403; permission_audit_logs denied=5 allowed=1.

tasks.expectation: Denied mutation does not create a generated artifact.

evidence.manualNote: Viewer route stress did not create a new artifact. After direct mutation attempts, the shared workspace remained at 8 artifacts, 8 generated-image artifacts, and 6 durable storage objects.
R95 preview screen run passed for a freshly created authenticated account. No Permission denied loop, no provider credential fault, and Benchmark A reached SUCCESS through the UI.

operatorRunbook: Open the preview URL through the browser profile used for the actor.
Use Workflow Pro Evidence bay as the current status dashboard.
Execute only the tasks listed for the current actor.
Record pass/fail, route status on failure, screenshot path or manual note, generated asset count, and download result.
Do not paste tokens, cookies, API keys, raw emails, or provider credentials into this file.
Run this script with --evidence <file> --strict after filling evidence to compute the score.

screenEvidence: Chrome UI signed up a fresh authenticated account on the latest preview deployment.
Workspace menu showed Workspace cloud session linked for the new owned workspace.
Workflow Pro loaded A / Input -> LLM -> LLM -> Output benchmark JSON from the screen.
Import Pasted JSON accepted the A contract with 4 nodes, 3 edges, and 1 output.
Apply Preview opened Graph with the A runtime graph.
Start All completed from the screen: input, both LLM nodes, output, and Workflow Runtime Lite all reached SUCCESS.

notes: Runtime events are best-effort under authenticated request-client fallback when service-role is absent.
Chrome still displayed one sync issue after the successful run; no failed sync_operations rows were found for the tested workspace/user pair.

screenEvidence: Owner actor was created and signed in from the Chrome screen on the R110 preview.
Workspace menu showed the owner role and the app resolved a writable workspace without permission loops.
Benchmark A was loaded from Workflow Pro, imported as pasted JSON, applied to Graph, and run from Start All; all A nodes and Runtime Lite reached SUCCESS.
Benchmark B was loaded from Workflow Pro, imported as pasted JSON, applied to Graph, and run from Start All; img2 produced a real 16:9 Standard image and Runtime Lite reached SUCCESS.
Benchmark C was loaded from Workflow Pro, imported as pasted JSON, applied to Graph, and run from Start All; all 13 nodes reached SUCCESS without imposing a hidden timeout.
C produced one seed image and three branch images; all three output nodes rendered generated images and the Graph header showed GENERATED 5.
Generated history listed the five generated-image records for the owner workspace.
Chrome/macOS downloaded the latest generated image from generated history, and the saved PNG was verified as 1536 x 864.

notes: Counts were verified with Supabase connector queries after the screen-operated owner run.
Generated image artifacts are exposed through /api/image-gen/assets while the asset bytes are backed by the nexus-generated-assets storage bucket.
The artifact rows do not yet expose an explicit storage metadata object, so future durability work should make the storage pointer first-class in artifact metadata.
No permission-denied loop, provider boundary denial, schema mismatch, sync payload overflow, or hidden runtime timeout was observed.

verification: Computer Use Chrome screen run for owner actor on the R110 preview
Benchmark A screen path: Load A -> Import Pasted JSON -> Apply Preview -> Graph -> Start All -> SUCCESS
Benchmark B screen path: Load B -> Import Pasted JSON -> Apply Preview -> Graph -> Start All -> real img2 image -> SUCCESS
Benchmark C screen path: Load C -> Import Pasted JSON -> Apply Preview -> Graph -> Start All -> 13/13 nodes SUCCESS
Generated history screen panel listed five generated-image records
Chrome/macOS generated-history download produced a 1536x864 PNG
Supabase connector verified artifacts=5, generated-image=5, storage objects=5, allowed audit=26, denied audit=0

screenEvidence: Signed in with a disposable authenticated test actor on the R99 preview.

Chrome UI showed SYNCED in the top-right toolbar after login.

backendTrace.workspaceStateStatusReason: Fresh workspace had no snapshot yet; this is not a permission failure.
notes: Pre-login 401s for the default local workspace are expected before authentication.
R99 prevents stale failed operations from another workspace from being counted against the active workspace toolbar status.
Generated media database traceability remains a separate follow-up.

screenEvidence: No browser actor was used in this round; this is a route-level source guard for the next screen matrix.

notes: This prepares the owner/editor/viewer screen matrix by pinning the API boundary first.
It does not replace real screen evidence for editor and viewer accounts.

screenEvidence: No viewer browser login was performed in this round; this is the runtime and database preflight that makes the viewer browser lane meaningful.
Preferred workspace session now accepts viewer memberships.
A viewer-only account with no writable workspace now resolves an existing readable workspace instead of creating a new owner workspace.

notes: This prevents viewer tests from accidentally landing in a newly created owner workspace.
The next screen run should create clean editor/viewer actors, attach them to the shared workspace, and validate behavior through Chrome.

screenEvidence: Clean viewer actor signed in from the Chrome screen on a fresh preview hostname.
Workspace menu showed ROLE: VIEWER for the shared workspace.
Top-right sync badge stayed SYNCED after role resolution; no post-login sync issue was created.
Graph showed VIEWER READ ONLY.
Add workflow nodes, Start All, delete agent, Rename, Spawn, Save, and Import were disabled in the visible UI.

notes: Pre-login 401s are expected before app authentication and are not counted as viewer permission failures.
Generated history inspection remains pending because the fresh preview displayed GENERATED 0 even though Supabase has generated-image rows.

screenEvidence: Editor generated a real img2 image on the final preview using standard quality and 16:9 ratio.
Viewer signed in on the same preview and shared workspace; workspace menu showed ROLE: VIEWER.
Viewer generated-history panel listed three generated-image records, including the new R108 durable storage verification image.
Viewer downloaded the newest generated-image record from the generated-history panel.
macOS saved the downloaded file as Generated-image---R108-durable-storage-verification-Y2K-streetwea.png with size 1,990,514 bytes.

notes: The new durable path uses Supabase Storage and /api/v1/artifacts/:artifactId/asset with workspace read permission checks.
Older generated-image records made before durable storage can still fail download in Chrome and are tracked as legacy debt.
No viewer mutation route was needed for the download; viewer only needs workspace read permission.
